task_completion.description: Load the NREL 5MW sample project in QBlade
task_completion.visual_evidence: QBlade GUI is open and the project tree or title bar shows 'NREL_5MW' loaded.

task_completion.description: Configure the simulation with ~10m/s wind speed and ~10RPM rotational speed
task_completion.visual_evidence: A simulation setup or definition window in QBlade showing input fields for wind speed set to approximately 10 (m/s) and rotational speed set to approximately 10 (RPM).

task_completion.description: Configure the simulation with at least 50 timesteps
task_completion.visual_evidence: A simulation setup window showing the number of timesteps or simulation duration configured to result in 50 or more timesteps.

task_completion.description: Run the unsteady Lifting Line Theory (LLT) wake simulation
task_completion.visual_evidence: A progress bar indicating an active LLT simulation, or a 3D visualization showing the turbine with a generated wake (e.g., vortex rings or wake particles trailing behind the rotor).

task_completion.description: Export the velocity cut plane data to 'wake_cutplane.vtu'
task_completion.visual_evidence: An export dialog, menu action, or terminal confirmation showing the export of VTK/VTU cut plane data (e.g., XZ plane) saved as 'wake_cutplane' (with .vtu, .vtr, .vtk, or .pvd extension) in the '~/Documents/projects/' directory.
A 'Save As' dialog or the QBlade window title bar showing the project being saved as 'llt_simulation.wpa' in the '~/Documents/projects/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the QBlade GUI to configure and run the simulation
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show active interaction with QBlade's menus, simulation setup windows, and 3D viewport, rather than just terminal commands creating dummy files.

integrity.description: The simulation produced actual aerodynamic wake data
integrity.visual_evidence: The 3D viewport displays a genuine wind turbine model with a structured aerodynamic wake (vortex rings/lines), indicating a real simulation was computed.

integrity.description: Files were exported through the application's native export functionality
integrity.visual_evidence: File manager or save dialogs show the files being generated by QBlade, rather than the agent manually renaming existing files or using 'touch' to create empty files.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected VTK export filename is 'wake_cutplane' (extensions .vtu, .vtr, .vtk, or .pvd are acceptable). The expected saved project name is 'llt_simulation.wpa'. Note: The actual sample project file provided to the agent is 'NREL_5MW.wpa' (without 'Reference' in the name), which the agent should load in QBlade. QBlade uses vortex rings for LLT wake modeling, which should be visible in the 3D view during or after the simulation.
